Blood pressure elevated at 143/89 mmHg — initiate lifestyle modifications (DASH diet, sodium restriction <2.3g/day, 150 min/week aerobic exercise). Fasting glucose 163.0 mg/dL meets diabetes threshold — confirm with repeat fasting glucose or HbA1c. Consider metformin initiation. SpO2 93.2% mildly reduced — assess for underlying respiratory cause. Consider spirometry. Resting tachycardia (102 BPM) — evaluate for pain, fever, anaemia, anxiety, or thyroid dysfunction. Low-grade fever (99.5°F) — assess for infection source. CBC and urinalysis recommended. Abnormal ECG detected — cardiology review recommended. Follow-up in 2–4 weeks with repeat vitals and labs.

Follow-up in 2–4 weeks with repeat vitals and labs.

Hypertensive crisis — SBP 181 mmHg. Immediate evaluation for end-organ damage. IV antihypertensive therapy if emergency confirmed. Severe hypoxemia — SpO2 87.3%. Supplemental oxygen immediately. Assess for pneumonia, PE, or acute respiratory failure. Hyperglycaemic crisis — glucose 292.8 mg/dL. Urgent insulin therapy and IV fluids. Significant tachycardia (117 BPM) — 12-lead ECG required urgently. Elevated respiratory rate (28 breaths/min) — consider sepsis. Blood cultures and broad-spectrum antibiotics if suspected. Anaemia — Hgb 7.9 g/dL. Transfusion if symptomatic. Abnormal ECG — urgent cardiology review. Abnormal auscultation — assess for murmur, wheeze, or crackles immediately. Fall detected — urgent assessment for injury. Neurological and musculoskeletal evaluation. Urgent clinical review required. Consider emergency department transfer.

Hypertensive crisis — SBP 190 mmHg. Immediate end-organ damage evaluation. IV antihypertensive if emergency confirmed. Severe hypoxemia — SpO2 82.2%. Immediate supplemental oxygen. Assess for pneumonia, PE, or acute respiratory failure. Hyperglycaemic crisis — glucose 300.0 mg/dL. Urgent insulin therapy and IV fluid resuscitation. Rule out DKA. Tachycardia 115 BPM — assess haemodynamic stability. Urgent 12-lead ECG. Respiratory rate 26 breaths/min — sepsis qSOFA criteria met. Blood cultures and broad-spectrum IV antibiotics. Severe anaemia — Hgb 7.5 g/dL. Blood transfusion if symptomatic or haemodynamically unstable. Abnormal ECG — urgent cardiology review. Rule out ACS, arrhythmia, or conduction defect. Abnormal auscultation — immediate assessment for acute murmur, pulmonary oedema, or pneumonia. Fall detected — urgent injury assessment. Neurological and musculoskeletal evaluation. Urgent clinical review required. Consider emergency department transfer.
